prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed with the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill. 

Method provenance
1. Property: Condensed (Mulliken-charge) Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor; identification of most electrophilic/nucleophilic atoms.
2. Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.x.
3. Geometry provenance: user-supplied geometry `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz`; single-point charges evaluated at this fixed structure (no re-optimization).
4. Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
5. Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet); N±1 states treated as doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2) per default protocol.
6. Software: chemkit v0.4 (backend PySCF 2.x, Mulliken charge analysis). Density fitting OFF (exact four-center integrals).
7. Convergence & integrity: all three SCF calculations (N, N-1, N+1) converged; integrity check status="ok", trustworthy=true.  Warnings reported by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Key numerical results (Mulliken scheme)
• Most electrophilic site (max f⁺): atom 4 (C, index 3)  f⁺ = 0.49641782
• Most nucleophilic site (max f⁻): atom 5 (C, index 4)  f⁻ = 0.10728820
• Dual descriptor Δf (f⁺ – f⁻) at atom 4: 0.51070986

The calculation’s live log was written during execution to the default `fukui_<timestamp>.out` file in the current working directory; the full result JSON is at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp_h2fcbyz/live.json`, and the bar-chart PNG is at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_o3/20260704-162906_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.

Verbatim warnings from the engine
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
3. common header present

The reported f⁺ value above (0.49641782) corresponds to the most electrophilic carbon (atom 4); all data originate from the computed DFT single-point charges and comply with chemkit’s calculation-reporting standards.
